petg_hf: Set Max Volumetric Speed to the value shown — Bambu Studio's default is too high for this material and causes stringing.

condition_warnings.message: {printer_name} has no enclosure. PETG prints better with stable ambient temperature. Consider a DIY enclosure or warm room to avoid layer separation.
PLA + enclosed printer + warm bed. Open the front door and remove the top glass panel to prevent heat creep and potential clogs.
PLA softens at ~57°C. For parts above 60°C, switch to PETG (Tg ~69°C), ASA (~100°C), or ABS (~98°C).
UV resistance: PLA and standard PETG degrade in prolonged sunlight. Consider ASA for outdoor applications.
Metallic / abrasive filaments require a hardened steel nozzle — standard brass/stainless wears rapidly.
For waterproofing: set wall loops to 4+ and infill to 60%+. PETG has better moisture and chemical resistance than PLA.
Glossy / Silk: PLA Silk produces the best gloss results. Slow outer wall to 40–50 mm/s and use fine layer height.
Matte finish: PLA Matte is designed for this — consider switching material for best results.
{material_name} is not AMS compatible. Multi-color printing requires an AMS-compatible filament.
High humidity: Dry your filament before printing — moisture causes bubbling, stringing, and weak layer bonding.
CF/GF filament detected. Hardened steel nozzle required — standard nozzles will wear out within hours.
0.2mm nozzle + CF/GF filament = immediate clog. Use 0.4mm minimum, 0.6mm recommended for carbon fiber materials.
